title: Miles Morales Actor Says a Venom Game Was Canceled—But Is That True?
summary:
Have you been waiting for a Venom video game? You're not alone! Nadji Jeter, the actor who plays Miles Morales in the Spider-Man games, just dropped some big news—or maybe not. In an interview, he said: "We were going to have a Venom game. We were going to drop a Venom game and a Venom DLC, but we lost Tony Todd…" Tony Todd was the actor who voiced Venom in Spider-Man 2, and he passed away in 2024.

But wait! A reporter named Jason Schreier says Jeter's story isn't true. He didn't explain exactly what he meant, but some people think the Venom game might still be happening. Back in 2023, leaked documents showed that Insomniac Games was working on a standalone Venom game, with a possible release in 2025. But now it's 2026, and no Venom game has been announced. Instead, Insomniac is busy making Marvel's Wolverine, coming this September.

So what's the truth? We'll have to wait and see. But one thing's for sure: fans really want a Venom game!
why_it_matters: This story shows that not everything you hear from actors or online is true, and it’s important to wait for official news before believing a rumor.

keywords.explanation: A video game about the villain Venom, who is a character from Spider-Man.
Extra content you can download for a video game, like new levels or characters.
An actor who voiced the character Venom in Spider-Man 2.

Who said Nadji Jeter’s story might not be true?

What character does Nadji Jeter play in the Spider-Man games?

background_read: In video games, companies sometimes release extra content called DLC (downloadable content) after the main game to add more fun.
Marvel's Spider-Man 2 came out in 2023 and featured Venom as a main villain, which made fans want a standalone Venom game.
Tony Todd was a famous actor known for deep voice roles, and he passed away in 2024.
Article_Structure: WHO: Nadji Jeter, the actor who plays Miles Morales in the Spider-Man games.
WHAT: He said a Venom game was canceled, but a reporter says that isn’t true.
WHERE: This news came out in interviews and online reports.
WHY: Fans are confused because there have been leaks about a Venom game, but no official announcement has been made.

perspectives.perspective: Fans who want a Venom game
perspectives.description: They are worried the game might be canceled, but some hold out hope based on the leaked documents.
He questions the actor’s statement and suggests the game might still be in development, showing that sources can be wrong.
They note that video game leaks often happen, but plans can change, so it’s best to wait for an official announcement.
